{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "SHIP",
    "one_liner": "Clean, correct guard-clause validation with no production risk — ship it."
  },
  "critical_findings": [],
  "important_issues": [],
  "suggestions": [
    "Consider making vehicleClass a union/enum type instead of raw string to catch typos at compile time (e.g., 'bike' vs 'Bike').",
    "If maxDeliveries could ever be 0 (courier suspended), the >= check already handles it correctly — but a dedicated isSuspended flag would make intent clearer.",
    "A minor readability win: extract the capacity check (currentDeliveries >= maxDeliveries) into a named helper like isAtCapacity() if this pattern repeats elsewhere."
  ],
  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "None — pure function with no side effects, no I/O, no state mutation.",
    "rollback_capability": "Trivial — stateless function, any prior version is a drop-in replacement.",
    "backward_compatibility": "No breaking changes; interface is additive-safe.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "N/A for a pure validation function; caller should log rejections if needed.",

}

content.thinking: Pure synchronous function, already validated data, simple boolean logic. No real production risks here.
